items.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
items.content: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

items.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
items.content: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

items.title: Apple touts $1.4 trillion in App Store billings and sales, 90% without a commission
items.content: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion last year, with $149 billion in sales for digital goods.

items.title: Apple’s MacBook Neo is winning over a new generation of buyers
items.content: The MacBook Neo shipped 1.1 million units in its first weeks on sale, IDC estimates, as Apple pushes deeper into the mainstream laptop market.

items.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
items.content: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

items.title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
items.content: Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.

items.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
items.content: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.

items.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
items.content: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

items.title: Flyover No More: Central Ohio Becomes Hub for Tech and Manufacturing
items.content: Tech titans and Silicon Valley transplants changed the Columbus area, but not everyone is thrilled about the rapid transformation.

items.title: Elon Musk Dominates List of Highest Paid C.E.O.s
items.content: Executive compensation is accelerating while rank-and-file workers lag, widening the pay gap in corporate America.

items.title: White House rejoices over strong jobs report.
items.content: The stronger-than-expected report offers President Trump a talking point for the midterms, even as it also reduces the odds that the Federal Reserve may cut rates.

items.title: More Renters Are Using Tools to Skip Security Deposits, but There’s a Catch
items.content: Third-party services charge fees to help renters bypass some upfront costs of signing a lease. Unlike traditional deposits, the money won’t be returned to tenants.
